title: GI function test
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c5dpc","text":"The GI function assay is used to assess GI function, including parameters like constipation, diarrhea and blood in stool.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"19tqp","text":"Stool collection assays are ideally performed between 9:00 am and 11:00 am on each day. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6eki","text":"Most feeding/defecation behavior occurs at night, so it is beneficial to run the experiment in a low light setting.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a7kpg","text":"Handling, room temperature and hour of the day impact results and should be maintained uniform along experimental days and experimental groups.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8o5ia","text":"Female mice pellet production is reduced if housed next to a male, therefore for the analysis females and males should be kept spatially isolated (different cages) to avoid odor influences.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9vhgo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8287f","text":"Materials:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"74lhe","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3cd0d","text":"Tripods","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4l5am","text":"Cameras with chargers","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13e1e","text":"Clean/spare cages","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bi90l","text":"Timer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4r65p","text":"Pens","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5sic7","text":"Extra cards for animal/cage ID","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1lt6o","text":"LabBook","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eg22i","text":"Labelled Eppendorf tubes and tube rack","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4u3tq","text":"Tweezer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d4qpc","text":"Cage lids for separation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Bring the mice to the analysis room 00:30:00 before analysis.
Remove each animal from its home cage and place in a clean plastic cage, with a lid, without food or water for 01:00:00 .
Take a picture of cage bottom for recording of pellet number.
Collect stools immediately and place in sealed tubes.

At the end of the trial, count and weigh the stools (total weight), then dry o/n ( 18:00:00 ) at 65 °C and weigh again to provide dry weight. Take a picture of the tubes (with the dry stools visible).

Calculate the water content as a difference between total weight and dry weight. Stool moisture content = [(A-B)/A] × 100 , where A is the weight of fresh stools collected after drug administration minus the average weight of an empty eppendorf tube (0.94 g), and B is the weight of stools after drying at 65 °C for 18:00:00 minus the average weight of an empty eppendorf tube (0.94 g). Incidence rate = Number of constipated mice/Total number of analyzed mice × 100.
Repeat the experiment for 2 times/week and take average values for statistical analysis per mouse.
After the testing time, transfer the mice back to their home cage.
creator: Hanne HD Dhondt
